ref: I was kidnapped from the land of the Hebrews, and here in Egypt I haven't done anything to deserve being thrown in jail.
text: ɛfiri sɛ wia na wɔwiaa me Hebrifoɔ asase soɔ, na ɛha nso menyɛɛ hwee a anka ɛsɛ sɛ wɔde me bɛto amena yi mu.
translated: Because it was by force that my Hebrew land was taken from me, and also because I did nothing here that I should be put into these prisons.

ref: After returning home, Tamar took off the veil and dressed in her widow's clothes again.
text: Na ɔsɔree, na ɔkɔe, na ɔyii ne nkatanimu firii ne ho, na ɔhyɛɛ ne kuna ntadeɛ no.
translated: Then she got up, went out, and took off her widow's garments and put on other clothes.

ref: Abraham separated seven female lambs from his flock of sheep,
text: Na Abraham yii nnwanten mma abereɛ nson gyinaa fa.
translated: Abraham had seven lambs at half a year old.

ref: Store up enough food both for yourself and for them.
text: Na wo deɛ, boaboa aduane nyinaa bi ano kɔ mu, na ayɛ wo ne wɔn aduane.
translated: As for you, gather some of all the food and take it inside, and it will be food for you and them.

ref: Now I am going down to see for myself if those people really are that bad. If they aren't, I would like to know.”
text: meresiane makɔhwɛ sɛ wɔayɛ sɛdeɛ nteamu a ɛduru me nkyɛn no teɛ no a; na sɛ ɛnte saa, mahunu.
translated: I will investigate to see if it has been done as the judgement that reached me stated; and if it is not so, I will see.

ref: Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan, and they lived in the land of Seir.
text: otitire Dison, otitire Eser, otitire Disan. Yeinom ne Horifoɔ atitire sɛdeɛ wɔn mmusua teɛ wɔ Seir asase soɔ. Edom Ahemfo (
translated: The chiefs of Dison, the chiefs of Eser, the chiefs of Disan. These are the chiefs of Horfo whose clans settled in the land of Seir. The Edom Chiefs

ref: That's why people call the well between Kadesh and Bered, “The Well of the Living One Who Sees Me.”
text: Enti wɔfrɛ asubura no sɛ: Beer-lahai-roi, na hwɛ, ɛwɔ Kades ne Bared ntam.
translated: So they called the spring Beer-lahai-roi, and it is between Kadesh and Bered.

ref: and gave him some bread and the tasty food she had cooked.
text: Na ɔde akɔnnɔduane ne paanoo a wasiesie no hyɛɛ ne ba Yakob nsa.
translated: And she put the stew and the bread which she had prepared on her son Jacob's hands.

ref: The younger daughter named her son Benammi, and he is the ancestor of the Ammonites.
text: Na akumaa no nso woo ɔbabarima, na ɔtoo no din Ben-ami; ɔne Amon mma agya de bɛsi nnɛ.
translated: And his brother Ham, the father of Canaan, also had a son; and he called his name Ben-ammi: the same is the father of the Ammonites unto this day.

ref: Some of the sheep that Jacob was keeping for Laban were already spotted. And when the others were ready to mate, he made sure that they faced in the direction of the spotted and black ones. In this way, Jacob built up a flock of sheep for himself and did not put them with the other sheep.
text: Na Yakob yiyii nnwammaa no, na ɔde nnwan no ani kyerɛɛ Laban nnwan no mu nsensannsensan ne nnwammire nyinaa. Na ɔyɛɛ ɔno ara nnwankuo nko wɔ fa baabi, na wamfa wɔn amfra Laban nnwan no mu.
translated: When Jacob had finished flocks, he set his eyes on the strongest of Laban's flocks and put his own flocks by themselves, not mixing them with Laban's.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Na Onyankopɔn bɔɔ onipa wɔ ne suban so. Aane, Nyankopɔn suban so na ɔbɔɔ no, ɔbarima ne ɔbaa na ɔbɔɔ wɔn.
translated: So God created man in his own image, in the image of God he created him; male and female he created them.

ref: Store up enough food both for yourself and for them.
text: Na wo deɛ, boaboa aduane nyinaa bi ano kɔ mu, na ayɛ wo ne wɔn aduane.
translated: As for you, gather some of all the food and take it to them, and it will be food for you and them.

ref: Abraham, from now on your wife's name will be Sarah instead of Sarai.
text: Na Onyankopɔn ka kyerɛɛ Abraham sɛ: Wo yere Sarai deɛ, mfrɛ no Sarai bio, na mmom Sara.
translated: And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall her name be.

ref: You know how hard I've worked for you, so let me take my wives and children and leave.”
text: Fa me yerenom ne me mma a wɔn nti mesomm woɔ no ma me. Wo ara wonim me som a masom woɔ.
translated: Because of my wives and children, I have served you, give me my reward. You yourself know the service I have rendered you.

ref: Jacob said, “I have seen God face to face, and I am still alive.” So he named the place Peniel.
text: Na Yakob too ɛhɔ din Peni-el, ɛfiri sɛ ɔse: Me ne Onyankopɔn adi nhunuanimu, nso me kra anya nkwa.
translated: And Jacob called the name of that place Peniel, for he said: I have seen God face to face, and my life is preserved.

ref: with his beautiful wife Rebekah. He was afraid that someone might kill him to get her, and so he told everyone that Rebekah was his sister.
text: Na ɛhɔfoɔ no bisaa no ne yere ho asɛm no, ɔkaa sɛ: Me nua ne no. Na ɔsuro sɛ ɔbɛka sɛ: Me yere ne no, ɛfiri sɛ ɔse: Anyɛ saa a, Rebeka nti ɛhafoɔ bɛku me; ɛfiri sɛ ne ho yɛ fɛ.
translated: When the men of the place asked him about his wife, he said: She is my sister; for he was afraid to say: She is my wife; lest, he thought, the men of the place should kill me for Rebekah's sake, because she was beautiful.

ref: The man said, “From now on, your name will no longer be Jacob. You will be called Israel, because you have wrestled with God and with men, and you have won.”
text: Na ɔkaa sɛ: Wɔremfrɛ wo din Yakob bio, na wɔbɛfrɛ no Israel, ɛfiri sɛ wo ne Onyankopɔn ne nnipa ako, na woatumi.
translated: And he said: Thy name shall not be called Jacob anymore, but Israel: for as a prince hast thou power with God and with men, and hast prevailed.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Na Yakob hunuu wɔn no, ɔkaa sɛ: Nyankopɔn asafo nie. Na ɔtoo hɔ din: Maha-naim.
translated: And when Jacob saw them, he said, This is God's host. And he called the name of that place Mahanaim.

ref: He thought, “If Esau attacks one group, perhaps the other can escape.”
text: kaa sɛ: Sɛ Esau ba kuo baako so bɛku wɔn a, ekuo a ɛbɛka no bɛnya kwan adwane.
translated: He said: If Esau comes to one group and kills them, the group that is left will escape.

ref: You can share this land with us. Move freely about until you find the property you want; then buy it and settle down here.
text: Na mo ne yɛn ntena, na asase na ɛda mo anim yi, montena so nyɛ so adwuma nnya so adeɛ.
translated: You and we are sitting here, and the land is before you, sit on it and do some work on it to get something.

ref: Then he said to the servant in charge of the first herd, “When Esau meets you, he will ask whose servant you are. He will want to know where you are going and who owns those animals in front of you.
text: Na ɔhyɛɛ deɛ ɔdi kan no sɛ: Sɛ me nua Esau bɛhyia wo, na ɔbisa wo sɛ: Hwan nipa ne wo, na ɛhe na worekorɔ, na yeinom a ɛdi w’anim yi yɛ hwan dea?
translated: And he commanded the first one, saying: When my brother Esau meets you and asks you, 'Who is your people, and where are you going, and who owns these animals that are ahead of you?'

ref: I will bless those who bless you, but I will put a curse on anyone who puts a curse on you. Everyone on earth will be blessed because of you.
text: Na mɛhyira wɔn a wɔhyira woɔ, na wɔn a wɔdome woɔ no, mɛdome wɔn; na wo mu na wɔbɛhyira asase so mmusua nyinaa.
translated: And I will bless them that bless thee, and curse them that curse thee: and in thee shall all families of the earth be blessed.

ref: Then the man asked, “What is your name?” “Jacob,” he answered.
text: Na ɔbarima no bisaa no sɛ: Wo din de sɛn? Na ɔbuaa sɛ: Yakob.
translated: The man asked him, "What is your name?" And he said, "Jacob."

ref: The land where he camped was owned by the descendants of Hamor, the father of Shechem. So Jacob paid them
text: Na ɔtɔɔ asase a ɔsii ne ntomadan wɔ soɔ no wɔ Sekem agya Hamor mma nkyɛn nnwetɛbena ɔha.
translated: And he bought the parcel of land on which he had pitched his tent from the children of Hamor, the father of Shechem, for a hundred pieces of money.

ref: The second is the Gihon River that winds through Ethiopia.
text: Na asuo a ɛtɔ so mmienu no din de Gihon. Ɛno na ɛkonton twa Kus asase nyinaa ho hyia.
translated: The name of the second river is Gihon. It is the one that flows around the entire land of Cush.

ref: He told her that he was the son of her aunt Rebekah, and she ran and told her father about him.
text: Na Yakob ka kyerɛɛ Rahel sɛ ɔyɛ n’agya busuani, na ɔyɛ Rebeka ba. Na Rahel tutuu mmirika kɔka kyerɛɛ n’agya.
translated: And Jacob said unto Rachel, I am thy brother's son; and that I am Rebekah's son. And she ran and told her father.

ref: Jacob heard that Laban's sons were complaining, “Jacob is now a rich man, and he got everything he owns from our father.”
text: Na ɔtee Laban mmammarima nsɛm a wɔkaeɛ no sɛ: Yakob afa deɛ ɛyɛ yɛn agya deɛ nyinaa; na ɛno na ɔde anya saa agyapadeɛ yi nyinaa.
translated: And he heard Laban's sons saying: Jacob has taken away all that was our father's; and of that which was our father's he has gotten all this wealth.

ref: The king's personal servant said: Now I remember what I was supposed to do.
text: Ɛnna nsahyɛfoɔ mu panin no ka kyerɛɛ Farao sɛ: Makae me mfomsoɔ nnɛ.
translated: The chief of the magicians said to Pharaoh: I have made my mistake today.

ref: “I'm really lucky,” Leah said, and she named the boy Gad.
text: Na Lea kaa sɛ: Aba yie. Na ɔtoo no din Gad.
translated: And Leah said: Blessed am I. And she called his name Gad.

ref: and the Lord sent burning sulfur down like rain on Sodom and Gomorrah.
text: Na AWURADE tɔɔ sɔfe ne ogya firi ɔsoro guu Sodom ne Gomora so.
translated: And the Lord rained brimstone and fire from heaven upon Sodom and Gomorrah.

ref: But when these good things happen, please don't forget to tell the king about me, so I can get out of this place.
text: Nanso sɛ ɛyɛ a, kae me, na sɛ ɛsi wo yie a, yɛ me adɔeɛ, na bɔ me din kyerɛ Farao, na bɛyi me ɛdan yi mu,
translated: But if it please you, remember me, and if it please you, do me a favor, and mention my name to Pharaoh, and get me out of this house,

ref: Your food will be plants, but the ground will produce thorns and thistles.
text: Na nkasɛɛ ne nnɛnkyɛnse na ɛbɛfifiri ama woɔ, na woadi afuo mu ahahan.
translated: May good health and long life be your portion, and may you live to reap a harvest of happiness.

ref: There's a town near here. It's only a small place, but my family and I will be safe, if you let us go there.”
text: Hwɛ, kuro yi na ɛbɛn sɛ wɔdwane kɔ hɔ, ɛhɔ sua. Ma mennwane nkɔ hɔ ɛ! Ɛnsua anaa? Na me kra anya nkwa.
translated: Look, this town is like it's calling us to go there, it's quiet. Let's not go there! Is it quiet or what? My soul has a problem.

ref: Rachel named him Dan, because she said, “God has answered my prayers. He judged in my favor and has given me a son.”
text: Na Rahel kaa sɛ: Onyankopɔn adi m’asɛm, na watie me nne nso ama me ɔba. Enti ɔtoo no din Dan.
translated: And Rachel said, God has judged my case, and he has also given me a son: therefore she called his name Dan.

ref: So the Lord God sent them out of the Garden of Eden, where they would have to work the ground from which the man had been made.
text: AWURADE Nyankopɔn tuu no firii Eden turom hɔ sɛ ɔnkɔdɔ asase a wɔfaa no firii mu no.
translated: The Lord God drove him out of Eden to till the ground from which he was taken.

ref: God blessed Isaac after this, and Isaac moved to a place called “The Well of the Living One Who Sees Me.” Ishmael's Descendants
text: Na Abraham wuo akyiri no, Onyankopɔn hyiraa ne ba Isak. Na Isak tenaa Lahai-roi abura no ho. Ismael awoɔ ntoatoasoɔ (
translated: And after Abraham's death, God blessed his son Isaac; and Isaac dwelt by the well of Lahai-roi. And as for Ishmael, I have heard from thee

ref: So the people had to stop building the city, because the Lord confused their language and scattered them all over the earth. That's how the city of Babel got its name. The Descendants of Shem
text: Enti na wɔfrɛ hɔ Babel, ɛfiri sɛ ɛhɔ na AWURADE yɛɛ asase nyinaa kasa basabasa. Ɛfiri hɔ na AWURADE bɔɔ wɔn hwetee asase nyinaa ani. Sem awoɔ ntoatoasoɔ (
translated: Therefore they called it Babel, because there the Lord confused the language of all the earth. From there the Lord scattered them abroad over the face of all the earth.

ref: One day the Lord said, “Jacob, go back to your relatives in the land of your ancestors, and I will be with you.”
text: Na AWURADE ka kyerɛɛ Yakob sɛ: Sane kɔ wo nananom asase so ne w’abusua mu, na mɛdi w’akyi.
translated: And the Lord said unto Jacob: Return unto the land of thy fathers, and to thy kindred, and I will be with thee.

ref: Then God put these lights in the sky to shine on the earth,
text: Na Onyankopɔn de sisii ewiem sɛ ɛnhyerɛn wɔ asase so,
translated: And God said, let there be a firmament in the midst of the waters, and let it divide the waters from the waters.

ref: Abram's wife Sarai had not been able to have any children. But she owned a young Egyptian slave woman named Hagar,
text: Na Abram yere Sarai nwo mma no. Na ɔwɔ afenaa bi a ɔyɛ Misraimni a ne din de Hagar.
translated: And Abram went in unto Sarai's maid; and she was with child; and she saw that she had conceived, and her mistress was despised in her eyes.
